name: Melt-in-Mouth Biscuits
recipeIngredient: 3 tbsp Solid vegetable shortening (or unsalted margarine)
1 cup All-purpose flour, for shaping biscuits
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 475°F (245°C). Spray an 8-inch baking pan with cooking spray.
2. In a medium bowl, mix together the flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and baking soda until well blended.
3. Cut in the shortening using a pastry blender or work it in with fingertips until the mixture forms small pea-sized lumps.
4. Stir in the buttermilk (or heavy cream) and let stand for 2-3 minutes. The dough will be very wet.
5. Place about 1 cup of all-purpose flour on a plate. Flour your hands well.
6. Scoop up about 2 tablespoons of dough and turn it onto the flour. Sprinkle more flour over the dough, then gently shape it into a soft round by passing it between your palms, shaking off excess flour. Place the biscuit in the center of the prepared pan.
7. Repeat with the remaining dough to form 9 biscuits.
8. Brush the tops of all biscuits with melted butter.
9. Bake for 16-18 minutes or until the tops are evenly browned.
These melt-in-your-mouth biscuits are best made with heavy cream instead of buttermilk. This recipe is from Woman's Day Magazine, February 22, 1994.
